# The Mayor of the Mantelpiece

It was the same joke in every town. Someone would ask Boxwell what office he
held, standing so straight beside the mantel clocks, and he would bow and
declare himself Mayor of the Mantelpiece, sworn defender of dust and chimes.
Children loved it. He never once said it twice the same way.

It was a joke. Everyone knew it was a joke. But it followed him from town to
town all the same.
